hyperedges.label: Foundation Governance Set: RFC-000 through RFC-005 explicitly form the completed foundational governance specification
Phase 0 Accepted Architecture Decisions: five ADRs grouped together as the accepted Phase 0 decision record
Local-First Principle repeated across product, engineering, design and architecture specification layers without direct cross-citation
Phase 4 AI Model and Policy Governance Gate
No-Ranking Ethics Guardrail Across Contract, UX and Test Plan
CI Verification Reports for executive-command-center PRs

nodes.label: Golden Rule: if it is not documented in the current phase, it does not get implemented
nodes.norm_label: golden rule: if it is not documented in the current phase, it does not get implemented

nodes.label: Spec-Code Sync Rule: behavior-changing code changes must update the specification in the same pull request
nodes.norm_label: spec-code sync rule: behavior-changing code changes must update the specification in the same pull request

nodes.label: Stop-and-Ask Protocol: implementation agent must stop and request a spec update rather than guess when the spec is ambiguous
nodes.norm_label: stop-and-ask protocol: implementation agent must stop and request a spec update rather than guess when the spec is ambiguous

nodes.label: AI Contributions Policy: AI-generated code welcome but must never invent APIs, requirements, technologies or architecture; reviewed like human code
nodes.norm_label: ai contributions policy: ai-generated code welcome but must never invent apis, requirements, technologies or architecture; reviewed like human code

nodes.label: GOV-001: The specification is authoritative; code implements the specification, never the reverse
nodes.norm_label: gov-001: the specification is authoritative; code implements the specification, never the reverse

nodes.label: GOV-002: Every architectural decision is documented
nodes.norm_label: gov-002: every architectural decision is documented

nodes.label: GOV-003: Every implementation traces back to requirements
nodes.norm_label: gov-003: every implementation traces back to requirements

nodes.label: GOV-004: Every behaviour-changing change updates documentation
nodes.norm_label: gov-004: every behaviour-changing change updates documentation

nodes.label: Specification Change Request (SCR): resolves ambiguity/missing requirements before implementation resumes
nodes.norm_label: specification change request (scr): resolves ambiguity/missing requirements before implementation resumes

nodes.label: Stop-and-Ask Rule: implementation SHALL stop on ambiguity, missing/conflicting requirements, undefined behaviour or security uncertainty; never guess
nodes.norm_label: stop-and-ask rule: implementation shall stop on ambiguity, missing/conflicting requirements, undefined behaviour or security uncertainty; never guess

nodes.label: Specification Freeze: frozen specifications receive Git tags (e.g. spec-v1.0-foundation) before implementation begins
nodes.norm_label: specification freeze: frozen specifications receive git tags (e.g. spec-v1.0-foundation) before implementation begins

nodes.label: Specification Drift: occurs when code changes but specification does not; CI SHALL reject such PRs
nodes.norm_label: specification drift: occurs when code changes but specification does not; ci shall reject such prs

nodes.label: Documentation Fitness Functions AFF-DOC-001..010: FR/test/ADR traceability, no drift, owned/versioned/changelogged docs, no circular dependencies
nodes.norm_label: documentation fitness functions aff-doc-001..010: fr/test/adr traceability, no drift, owned/versioned/changelogged docs, no circular dependencies

nodes.label: RFC-001: Executive Command Center Product Definition
nodes.norm_label: rfc-001: executive command center product definition

nodes.label: Product Principle 1 - Local First: user data belongs to user; system MUST function locally, cloud is optional and never mandatory
nodes.norm_label: product principle 1 - local first: user data belongs to user; system must function locally, cloud is optional and never mandatory

nodes.label: Product Principle 2 - Human Decision Making: AI assists, humans decide; system MUST recommend and explain, MUST NOT silently make executive decisions
nodes.norm_label: product principle 2 - human decision making: ai assists, humans decide; system must recommend and explain, must not silently make executive decisions

nodes.label: Product Principle 3 - Explainability: every recommendation must answer why, based on what, which evidence, what confidence
nodes.norm_label: product principle 3 - explainability: every recommendation must answer why, based on what, which evidence, what confidence

nodes.label: Product Principle 4 - Long-Term Memory: executives forget, software should not; memory is permanent, summaries are disposable
nodes.norm_label: product principle 4 - long-term memory: executives forget, software should not; memory is permanent, summaries are disposable

nodes.label: Product Principle 5 - Attention Is The Product: the scarce resource is executive focus, not information; every feature must improve attention allocation
nodes.norm_label: product principle 5 - attention is the product: the scarce resource is executive focus, not information; every feature must improve attention allocation

nodes.label: Product Principle 6 - Progressive Automation: Observe -> Recommend -> Assist -> Automate -> Autonomous; must begin with recommendation, trust is earned
nodes.norm_label: product principle 6 - progressive automation: observe -> recommend -> assist -> automate -> autonomous; must begin with recommendation, trust is earned

nodes.label: Functional Requirements FR-001..FR-010: dashboard, consolidation, commitments, priorities, memory, relationships, meeting prep, follow-ups, approved scheduling, explainability
nodes.norm_label: functional requirements fr-001..fr-010: dashboard, consolidation, commitments, priorities, memory, relationships, meeting prep, follow-ups, approved scheduling, explainability

nodes.label: Non-Functional Requirements NFR-001..NFR-006: no mandatory cloud AI, 2s dashboard load, offline usability, local data ownership, incremental expansion, replaceable subsystems
nodes.norm_label: non-functional requirements nfr-001..nfr-006: no mandatory cloud ai, 2s dashboard load, offline usability, local data ownership, incremental expansion, replaceable subsystems

nodes.label: Product Maturity Model: Capture -> Organize -> Understand -> Recommend -> Reason -> Collaborate (Stage 1 Executive Assistant through Stage 5 Executive Operating System)
nodes.norm_label: product maturity model: capture -> organize -> understand -> recommend -> reason -> collaborate (stage 1 executive assistant through stage 5 executive operating system)

nodes.label: EP-001 Specification Before Code: development follows Understand->Specify->Review->Implement->Test->Deploy; no behavior-changing PR without spec update
nodes.norm_label: ep-001 specification before code: development follows understand->specify->review->implement->test->deploy; no behavior-changing pr without spec update

nodes.label: EP-002 Simplicity Wins: the simplest solution satisfying a requirement SHALL be preferred; complexity compounds
nodes.norm_label: ep-002 simplicity wins: the simplest solution satisfying a requirement shall be preferred; complexity compounds

nodes.label: EP-003 Delete Before Add: evaluate existing systems before introducing new ones; adding code is easy, removing is difficult
nodes.norm_label: ep-003 delete before add: evaluate existing systems before introducing new ones; adding code is easy, removing is difficult

nodes.label: EP-004 AI is a Junior Engineer: AI accelerates but does not replace engineering judgment; every AI change needs human review, architectural validation, tests, traceability
nodes.norm_label: ep-004 ai is a junior engineer: ai accelerates but does not replace engineering judgment; every ai change needs human review, architectural validation, tests, traceability

nodes.label: EP-005 Local First: local execution is default for privacy, ownership, latency, resilience, cost; cloud SHALL NOT be mandatory for core workflows
nodes.norm_label: ep-005 local first: local execution is default for privacy, ownership, latency, resilience, cost; cloud shall not be mandatory for core workflows

nodes.label: EP-006 Explainability Over Intelligence: an explainable recommendation beats an opaque one; unexplainable recommendations SHOULD NOT be shown
nodes.norm_label: ep-006 explainability over intelligence: an explainable recommendation beats an opaque one; unexplainable recommendations should not be shown

nodes.label: EP-007 Human Authority: AI recommends, humans decide; AI SHALL NEVER silently send email, delete data, modify data, execute workflows or approve requests
nodes.norm_label: ep-007 human authority: ai recommends, humans decide; ai shall never silently send email, delete data, modify data, execute workflows or approve requests

nodes.label: EP-008 Permanent Memory: information should never be intentionally discarded; system prefers preservation over deletion; summaries regenerate, memory persists
nodes.norm_label: ep-008 permanent memory: information should never be intentionally discarded; system prefers preservation over deletion; summaries regenerate, memory persists

nodes.label: EP-009 Modular Architecture: subsystems have one responsibility, communicate through contracts; dependency direction UI->Application->Domain->Infrastructure, never reversed
nodes.norm_label: ep-009 modular architecture: subsystems have one responsibility, communicate through contracts; dependency direction ui->application->domain->infrastructure, never reversed

nodes.label: EP-010 Evolution Over Perfection: choose solutions that evolve safely rather than predicting every future requirement; stability over perfection
nodes.norm_label: ep-010 evolution over perfection: choose solutions that evolve safely rather than predicting every future requirement; stability over perfection

nodes.label: Architectural Fitness Functions AFF-001..010: LLM calls via Model Router, connectors never touch DBs directly, contract-based agent comms, auditable AI actions, FR traceability, no business logic in UI, no secrets in source, versioned prompts, subsystem health, reversible changes
nodes.norm_label: architectural fitness functions aff-001..010: llm calls via model router, connectors never touch dbs directly, contract-based agent comms, auditable ai actions, fr traceability, no business logic in ui, no secrets in source, versioned prompts, subsystem health, reversible changes

nodes.label: Technical Debt Philosophy: debt is not forbidden, unmanaged/invisible debt is; every shortcut must record reason, impact, owner, review date, removal plan
nodes.norm_label: technical debt philosophy: debt is not forbidden, unmanaged/invisible debt is; every shortcut must record reason, impact, owner, review date, removal plan

nodes.label: DP-001 Attention Is The Primary Resource: UI must prioritize action, decisions, risks, commitments and deprioritize stats/vanity metrics/decorative widgets
nodes.norm_label: dp-001 attention is the primary resource: ui must prioritize action, decisions, risks, commitments and deprioritize stats/vanity metrics/decorative widgets

nodes.label: DP-002 Local First: local execution is default architecture for privacy, speed, ownership, offline capability, lower cost; cloud extends but never owns the product
nodes.norm_label: dp-002 local first: local execution is default architecture for privacy, speed, ownership, offline capability, lower cost; cloud extends but never owns the product

nodes.label: DP-003 Explain Everything: every recommendation should answer why, what evidence, how confident, what happens if ignored
nodes.norm_label: dp-003 explain everything: every recommendation should answer why, what evidence, how confident, what happens if ignored

nodes.label: DP-004 Memory Is Permanent: ECC continuously builds richer understanding of people, projects, orgs, commitments, meetings, decisions; deleting context is exceptional
nodes.norm_label: dp-004 memory is permanent: ecc continuously builds richer understanding of people, projects, orgs, commitments, meetings, decisions; deleting context is exceptional

nodes.label: DP-005 Progressive Disclosure: users see only what is required now (Dashboard->Project->Meeting->Conversation->Original Email); present maximum relevance not maximum information
nodes.norm_label: dp-005 progressive disclosure: users see only what is required now (dashboard->project->meeting->conversation->original email); present maximum relevance not maximum information

nodes.label: DP-006 Calm Interfaces: avoid blinking, animations, unnecessary colors, repeated alerts, graph-filled dashboards; whitespace, silence and focus are features
nodes.norm_label: dp-006 calm interfaces: avoid blinking, animations, unnecessary colors, repeated alerts, graph-filled dashboards; whitespace, silence and focus are features

nodes.label: DP-007 Relationships Over Documents: ECC organizes around Meeting->People->Decisions->Projects->Action Items->Risks rather than files; documents are evidence, relationships are knowledge
nodes.norm_label: dp-007 relationships over documents: ecc organizes around meeting->people->decisions->projects->action items->risks rather than files; documents are evidence, relationships are knowledge

nodes.label: DP-008 Context Before Content: every piece of information is accompanied by participants, previous meeting, related decisions, risks, documents, action items
nodes.norm_label: dp-008 context before content: every piece of information is accompanied by participants, previous meeting, related decisions, risks, documents, action items

nodes.label: DP-009 Human In Control: AI recommends, humans decide; all state-changing actions (send email, delete, schedule, delegate, update tasks) require explicit confirmation
nodes.norm_label: dp-009 human in control: ai recommends, humans decide; all state-changing actions (send email, delete, schedule, delegate, update tasks) require explicit confirmation

nodes.label: DP-010 AI Should Feel Invisible: users should experience outcomes, not AI; good AI is quiet infrastructure, not the product
nodes.norm_label: dp-010 ai should feel invisible: users should experience outcomes, not ai; good ai is quiet infrastructure, not the product

nodes.label: DP-011 Executive Dashboard First: every widget must answer what needs attention, what changed, what is blocked, what is at risk, what should happen next
nodes.norm_label: dp-011 executive dashboard first: every widget must answer what needs attention, what changed, what is blocked, what is at risk, what should happen next

nodes.label: DP-012 Reduce Decisions: prefer automatic classification/linking/organization/prioritization over manual folder/category/tag/label choices
nodes.norm_label: dp-012 reduce decisions: prefer automatic classification/linking/organization/prioritization over manual folder/category/tag/label choices

nodes.label: DP-013 Search Is A Backup: information should arrive when relevant; search exists for recovery, not primary navigation
nodes.norm_label: dp-013 search is a backup: information should arrive when relevant; search exists for recovery, not primary navigation

nodes.label: DP-014 Time Is A First-Class Entity: ECC stores timelines not files; everything answers when, what changed, before/after
nodes.norm_label: dp-014 time is a first-class entity: ecc stores timelines not files; everything answers when, what changed, before/after

nodes.label: DP-015 Design For Years: every feature must scale to 100k emails, 20k meetings, 5k documents, 50k tasks or be redesigned
nodes.norm_label: dp-015 design for years: every feature must scale to 100k emails, 20k meetings, 5k documents, 50k tasks or be redesigned

nodes.label: DP-016 Small Surfaces: interfaces expose only a few primary actions; configuration is not capability
nodes.norm_label: dp-016 small surfaces: interfaces expose only a few primary actions; configuration is not capability

nodes.label: DP-017 One Source Of Truth: every concept has exactly one owner - Calendar->Calendar Service, Tasks->Task Engine, Knowledge->Knowledge Graph, Models->Model Router
nodes.norm_label: dp-017 one source of truth: every concept has exactly one owner - calendar->calendar service, tasks->task engine, knowledge->knowledge graph, models->model router

nodes.label: DP-018 Consistency Over Novelty: reuse interaction patterns, layouts, terminology; novelty increases learning cost
nodes.norm_label: dp-018 consistency over novelty: reuse interaction patterns, layouts, terminology; novelty increases learning cost

nodes.label: DP-019 Defaults Matter: every default represents the recommended path; zero-configuration onboarding is the long-term goal
nodes.norm_label: dp-019 defaults matter: every default represents the recommended path; zero-configuration onboarding is the long-term goal

nodes.label: DP-020 Every Screen Must Earn Its Place: before adding a screen ask if it could be integrated, contextual, automatic, or eliminated
nodes.norm_label: dp-020 every screen must earn its place: before adding a screen ask if it could be integrated, contextual, automatic, or eliminated

nodes.label: Design Heuristics DH-001..008: simpler, fewer clicks, AI-eliminable, context-removable, 5-second clarity, automatable, explainable, durable over 5 years
nodes.norm_label: design heuristics dh-001..008: simpler, fewer clicks, ai-eliminable, context-removable, 5-second clarity, automatable, explainable, durable over 5 years

nodes.label: RFC-004 Architectural Rules: explicit domain ownership, versioned service contracts, business state owned by domains not AI agents, all model access via Model Router, connectors never write domain DBs directly, no cross-domain DB access, state-changing AI actions need human approval, local execution default
nodes.norm_label: rfc-004 architectural rules: explicit domain ownership, versioned service contracts, business state owned by domains not ai agents, all model access via model router, connectors never write domain dbs directly, no cross-domain db access, state-changing ai actions need human approval, local execution default

nodes.label: ADR-0001 Repository Layout: monorepo with docs/backend/frontend/packages/tests/scripts/infrastructure/.github; rejected multi-repo (fragments context) and framework-first folders (weakens domain ownership)
nodes.norm_label: adr-0001 repository layout: monorepo with docs/backend/frontend/packages/tests/scripts/infrastructure/.github; rejected multi-repo (fragments context) and framework-first folders (weakens domain ownership)

nodes.label: ADR-0002 Local-First Architecture: core workflows, storage, search, scheduling and default AI path operate locally; cloud is an optional adapter, never mandatory; rejected SaaS-first and offline-cache-with-cloud-authority for conflicting with product principles / subordinating local operation
nodes.norm_label: adr-0002 local-first architecture: core workflows, storage, search, scheduling and default ai path operate locally; cloud is an optional adapter, never mandatory; rejected saas-first and offline-cache-with-cloud-authority for conflicting with product principles / subordinating local operation

nodes.label: ADR-0003 PKOS (Personal Knowledge Operating System): canonical knowledge subsystem storing normalized entities, typed relationships, provenance, temporal validity; source artifacts remain immutable evidence, derived summaries/embeddings are replaceable projections; rejected vector-DB-as-primary-memory (no authoritative relationships) and notes-only model (no cross-domain reasoning)
nodes.norm_label: adr-0003 pkos (personal knowledge operating system): canonical knowledge subsystem storing normalized entities, typed relationships, provenance, temporal validity; source artifacts remain immutable evidence, derived summaries/embeddings are replaceable projections; rejected vector-db-as-primary-memory (no authoritative relationships) and notes-only model (no cross-domain reasoning)

nodes.label: ADR-0004 AI Runtime: all model calls pass through a single AI Runtime (Model Router, prompt registry, structured-output validation, tool permission checks, evaluation hooks, audit logging); AI outputs are proposals until validated; rejected direct model calls per feature for fragmenting policy/evaluation/observability
nodes.norm_label: adr-0004 ai runtime: all model calls pass through a single ai runtime (model router, prompt registry, structured-output validation, tool permission checks, evaluation hooks, audit logging); ai outputs are proposals until validated; rejected direct model calls per feature for fragmenting policy/evaluation/observability

nodes.label: Model Router: centralized component through which all LLM/model calls must pass (provider routing, local/cloud selection, auditable versions)
nodes.norm_label: model router: centralized component through which all llm/model calls must pass (provider routing, local/cloud selection, auditable versions)

nodes.label: ADR-0005 Event Bus: versioned immutable domain events, past-tense named, standard envelope, published after commit, idempotent consumers, at-least-once delivery, ordering only within an aggregate stream; Phase 0 uses in-process durable implementation behind an event-bus contract; rejected direct service-to-service calls for synchronous coupling/cascading failure
nodes.norm_label: adr-0005 event bus: versioned immutable domain events, past-tense named, standard envelope, published after commit, idempotent consumers, at-least-once delivery, ordering only within an aggregate stream; phase 0 uses in-process durable implementation behind an event-bus contract; rejected direct service-to-service calls for synchronous coupling/cascading failure

nodes.label: Chapter 2B: Platform Runtime, Deployment & Operational Architecture
nodes.norm_label: chapter 2b: platform runtime, deployment & operational architecture

nodes.label: ADR-0006: Storage Strategy
nodes.rationale: PostgreSQL chosen as the sole canonical transactional store for Phase 0 (with content-addressed object storage and pgvector for retrieval) to keep one operational database and simple backup semantics while ECC remains local-first. A dedicated graph engine or search service was deliberately deferred pending measured limitations. Multiple databases from day one were rejected as premature operational complexity.

nodes.label: ADR-0007: Model Router
nodes.rationale: A provider-neutral Model Router was introduced as the sole inference entry point because different tasks need different models, privacy levels, latency and cost profiles; local models are preferred for sensitive content and fallback to cloud must be explicit and auditable, never silent. Feature-owned model selection was rejected because it duplicates policy and increases privacy risk.

nodes.label: ADR-0008: Authentication and Workspace Identity
nodes.rationale: Even though ECC begins as a single local-user system, workspace_id/owner_id and full auth enforcement were required from Phase 0 so no unsafe no-auth default is embedded and later multi-user isolation is not expensive to retrofit. Opaque server-side sessions (not JWT) were chosen because revocation/rotation is simpler for a local modular monolith. No-auth mode, JWT sessions, and OAuth-only login were all explicitly rejected.

nodes.label: ADR-0009: Connector Synchronization
nodes.rationale: Connectors use incremental cursor-based sync with idempotent imports and preserved source artifacts because external systems remain authoritative while ECC needs safe re-sync/replay and full provenance; conflicts are recorded and surfaced rather than silently resolved. Full replacement syncs were rejected as costly, fragile and poor at preserving deletion history.

nodes.label: ADR-0010: Deployment Strategy
nodes.rationale: Phase 0 ships as a containerized modular monolith (frontend, backend, worker, PostgreSQL, optional Ollama) via Docker Compose, with domain boundaries kept as internal contracts rather than network boundaries, to keep local setup and debugging simple. Kubernetes and independently deployed microservices in Phase 0 were rejected as premature complexity; microservice extraction requires measured need and a new ADR.

nodes.label: Merge and Split
nodes.rationale: No authoritative source record is ever deleted during a merge; reversal restores prior identities unless a later dependent operation requires manual split. This preserves auditability and reversibility of identity decisions instead of allowing silent, destructive collapsing of distinct entities.

nodes.label: Excluded Attention Inputs
nodes.rationale: Protected characteristics, inferred emotion or personality, employee activity volume, message-response speed as a performance proxy, and private-source content without permission are excluded from the score to prevent it from becoming a covert surveillance or performance-ranking tool.

nodes.label: Optional AI Enrichment
nodes.rationale: AI summarization of retrieved evidence is feature-flagged and kept clearly separate from the deterministic pack so the meeting pack stays trustworthy and available even when AI is disabled or introduces uncited facts.

nodes.label: Planning Conflict Handling
nodes.rationale: Over-capacity, missed-deadline and hard-constraint conflicts are always surfaced explicitly rather than silently resolved or hidden, so the human stays in control of trade-offs instead of the planner silently violating a hard constraint.

nodes.label: Attention UX Ethics and Accessibility Constraints
nodes.rationale: Red/green-only status cues, urgency animation, shame language and person leaderboards are prohibited to avoid gamifying or shaming human attention, keeping the tool a decision aid rather than a surveillance or ranking system.
nodes.norm_label: attention ux ethics and accessibility constraints

nodes.label: Evaluation Promotion Blocking Criteria
nodes.rationale: Any privacy leak, unsupported consequential claim, unauthorized tool request or critical regression blocks promotion, ensuring only safety-evaluated policy/model/prompt changes reach production and online feedback alone cannot silently update policy.

nodes.label: Deterministic Routing Eligibility
nodes.rationale: Local models are preferred when they meet the approved quality floor and remote provider use requires explicit configuration, prioritizing data residency and privacy over raw quality or cost preference; a caller cannot override policy or enable an unapproved provider.

nodes.label: Failure Never Blocks Deterministic Core Flow
nodes.norm_label: failure never blocks deterministic core flow

nodes.label: Local Dev Bootstrap Environment Guard
nodes.rationale: Local dev bootstrap restricts execution to the development environment and rejects remote/non-development database targets by default, only permitting an explicit opt-in for a remote development database. The design intent is to prevent an accidental bootstrap run from wiping or reseeding a shared, staging, or production database, and to keep the bootstrap page hidden outside development with strict response headers on a fragment-based route.

nodes.label: Mypy Static Type-Check CI Gate
nodes.rationale: PR7 enforces static type checking via mypy across all 26 source files as a CI verification step, catching type errors before merge to keep the backend's type contracts sound.
